defender-session-baseline.description: Authenticated Defender XDR session baseline used across apiproxy calls, shell bootstrap, and same-origin portal helpers.

principalContext.label: Global Admin
notes: Observed with a Global Admin account holding broad administrative privileges.
Effective permissions, token claims, and route access can vary for users with narrower roles.

cookies.name: sccauth
cookies.description: Defender XDR portal session cookie required for apiproxy and same-origin portal requests.
notes: Raw cookie values must never be stored in committed specs or generated ledgers.

cookies.name: XSRF-TOKEN
cookies.description: Anti-forgery cookie paired with the X-XSRF-TOKEN request header on same-origin and multi-tenant Defender requests.
notes: The live portal URL-decodes this cookie into the X-XSRF-TOKEN request header when required.
Raw cookie values must never be stored in committed specs or generated ledgers.

permissionSignals.name: shell-info
notes: Shell bootstrap responses expose current portal configuration, user context, and enabled surfaces before feature blades fully hydrate.

defender-mto-tenant-context.description: Tenant-selection and cross-tenant routing context reused by the Defender multi-tenant management portal.
notes: MTO requests can depend on the Defender session plus an X-XSRF-TOKEN header and mtoproxyurl=MTO routing hint.

principalContext.label: Global Admin
notes: Observed with a Global Admin account holding broad administrative privileges.
Effective permissions and visible tenant scope can vary for users with narrower roles.

permissionSignals.name: tenant-picker
notes: Tenant picker responses enumerate the accessible tenant set and effective selection context for the signed-in operator.

permissionSignals.name: effective-tenant-group
notes: Effective tenant-group responses show which managed-tenant grouping the current operator is actively scoped to across MTO experiences.

permissionSignals.name: tenant-context-bootstrap
notes: Tenant-context bootstrap responses expose the selected tenant, workload state, and MTO execution metadata used before downstream pages load.

defender-copilot-entitlement.description: Security Copilot entitlement and readiness checks surfaced through Defender portal service helpers.

notes: Observed with a Global Admin account holding broad administrative privileges.

permissionSignals.name: medeina-auth
notes: Medeina auth responses indicate whether Security Copilot authentication and startup prerequisites are satisfied for the current session.

permissionSignals.name: security-copilot-trial
notes: Trial responses expose tenant-level onboarding and entitlement state for Security Copilot experiences.

permissionSignals.name: mto-security-copilot-trial
notes: The MTO wrapper probes Security Copilot readiness from multi-tenant hunting and detection experiences and can return contextual proxy-prefix validation errors.

notes: Returned tenant choices and the effective selection depend on the signed-in operator and available MTO scope.

requestShapes.name: cached-or-realtime-bootstrap

operations.summary: Get MTO Security Copilot trial status

workflows: Open MTO Advanced hunting or Custom detection experiences that probe Security Copilot readiness.

requestShapes.name: scoped-trial-probe
requestShapes.notes: The portal submits an access-scope query value while checking multi-tenant Security Copilot readiness.

notes: Trial readiness depends on tenant onboarding, current scope, and whether the MTO wrapper accepts the requested proxy prefix.
Live traffic in the current tenant returned a contextual 400 InvalidProxyPrefix response, confirming the probe path and parameter shape.

workflows: Load or refresh the Defender XDR shell before feature blades and cross-solution entry points fully hydrate.

notes: Returned shell state reflects the signed-in operator, current tenant, and enabled Defender surfaces.

workflows: Open Security Copilot entry points or related Defender experiences that check Copilot readiness.

notes: Returned auth and readiness state depends on tenant enablement and the signed-in operator.

workflows: Load trial, onboarding, or entitlement UX for Security Copilot from the Defender portal.

notes: Trial state is specific to the current tenant and may be absent where Security Copilot onboarding is disabled.

operations.summary: Get Office on the Web settings

operationContext.surface: Org settings > Services > Microsoft 365 on the web

notes: The clickable blade routed to `#/Settings/Services/:/Settings/L1/OfficeOnline` during live capture.
A direct `#/Settings/Services/:/Settings/L1/OfficeOnTheWeb` hash fell back to `#/homepage`, so treat this path as a documented alias until new live evidence appears.
The checked-in M365Internals helper resolves this surface to /admin/api/settings/apps/officeonline.
Live navigation clicked the Microsoft 365 on the web row and the resulting request used `/admin/api/settings/apps/officeonline`.

viva-engage-graphql.description: Same-origin Viva Engage GraphQL profile confirmed by the authenticated admin landing capture.
notes: No `Cookie`, `Authorization-Receiver`, or `X-Yam-*` request headers were observed on the captured authenticated admin GraphQL calls for this pass.

The shipped bundle code can also emit `X-Yam-Scope-Type`, `X-Yam-TenantId`, and `X-Yam-Scope-Entity-Id` when a request is scoped to a Teams meeting context, but those headers were not present on the admin landing requests captured here.
Preserve raw token strings and cookie values outside committed specs and generated ledgers.

requiredHeaders.name: Authorization
requiredHeaders.description: Browser-issued bearer token acquired for the Viva Engage or Yammer resource scope.

observedHeaders.name: Content-Type
observedHeaders.description: Observed on the authenticated persisted GraphQL POST requests.

observedHeaders.name: Referer
observedHeaders.description: Admin-surface referer used by the authenticated admin landing flow.

observedHeaders.name: X-Request-Id
observedHeaders.description: Client-generated request correlation header emitted on the captured same-origin GraphQL requests.

observedHeaders.name: X-Analytics-Client-Properties
observedHeaders.description: Client telemetry context header attached to the authenticated GraphQL calls.

observedHeaders.name: X-ECS-Etag
observedHeaders.description: Configuration snapshot header attached to the authenticated GraphQL calls.

viva-engage-token-helper.description: Cross-host token-helper profile confirmed during the authenticated Viva Engage admin landing capture.
notes: No `Cookie`, `Authorization-Receiver`, or `X-Yam-*` request headers were observed on the captured helper call for this pass.

The shipped bundle code also supports an optional `X-Yammer-ThirdPartyCookieBlocked` header and requests the helper with `credentials: include`, even though the captured admin request still showed no cookie header.
Preserve raw token strings and cookie values outside committed specs and generated ledgers.

requiredHeaders.name: Authorization
requiredHeaders.description: Browser-issued bearer token reused for the token-status helper request.

observedHeaders.name: Referer
observedHeaders.description: Root Engage referer observed on the helper request.

observedHeaders.name: X-Yammer-OAuthTokenExpiration
observedHeaders.description: Token-expiration helper header attached to the cross-host bearer-backed request.

viva-engage-realtime-relay.description: Transient `*.rt.yammer.com` CometD relay profile derived from the authenticated Viva Engage admin shell.
notes: The captured realtime relay requests did not use `Authorization`, `Cookie`, `X-Request-Id`, or `X-Yam-*` request headers.
Authentication was carried in the Bayeux handshake payload through redacted `ext.token` and `ext.hub_tenant_token` values.
The relay host was supplied by the same-origin `RealtimeConnectionSettingsClients` GraphQL response and changed across page loads.

observedHeaders.name: Referer
observedHeaders.description: Root Engage referer observed on the realtime relay requests.

observedHeaders.name: Content-Type
observedHeaders.description: Observed on the initial Bayeux handshake request. Later subscribe and connect posts did not consistently add extra custom request headers.

operations.summary: Execute Viva Engage admin GraphQL operations

workflows: Open `https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/admin`, allow the Viva Engage admin shell to authenticate through MSAL, and wait for the landing view to resolve its persisted GraphQL calls.
Open `https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/admin/segmentation` to load the dedicated segmentation admin page and its persisted GraphQL bootstrap query.
Open `https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/admin/external-networks-settings` to load the canonical external-networks admin page.
Open `https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/admin/setup-external-network` to continue into the external-network setup flow, which reused the external-networks admin bootstrap query during this pass.
Preserve the `RealtimeConnectionSettingsClients` response if present, because it provides the transient `cometdBaseUrl` used by the Yammer-era realtime relay chain.

notes: The authenticated landing capture confirmed 27 unique persisted query names or aliases, but the exact operation mix remains tenant- and role-dependent.

The admin landing pass confirmed operations such as `CurrentUserClients`, `FeatureAccessSettingsClients`, `TenantConfigurationAndRoles`, `NavigationGroupsClients`, and `RealtimeConnectionSettingsClients`.
The segmentation direct-route capture confirmed `NetworkSegmentationQueryClients` together with the dedicated `4-page-admin-segmentation-settings.js` route chunk.
The external-networks settings and setup direct-route captures confirmed `ExternalNetworksAdminSettingsClients` together with the dedicated `4-page-admin-external-networks-settings.js` route chunk.

The `RealtimeConnectionSettingsClients` operation returned transient `cometdBaseUrl` values that bootstrapped `*.rt.yammer.com/cometd/handshake`, `/cometd/`, and `/cometd/connect` relay calls without `Authorization` or cookie headers.

Navigating to `/main/admin/external-networks` updated the URL but rendered the generic admin landing state during this pass, so `external-networks-settings` should be treated as the canonical external-networks route.
The default Edge profile captured persisted GraphQL POST requests after the Viva Engage admin shell hydrated successfully.
Direct-route captures validated the segmentation, external-networks settings, and setup-external-network admin pages in the authenticated default Edge profile.
The checked-in bundle set exposed `https://engage.cloud.microsoft/graphql` as the configured GraphQL host.

operations.summary: Probe Viva Engage AAD access-token state

workflows: Open `https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/admin` and allow the shell bootstrap flow to initialize cross-host auth helpers after MSAL sign-in completes.

notes: The authenticated landing capture observed this bearer-backed GET request without a request body.
The browser aborted the helper response after headers were received, so the response body was not retained in the capture artifacts.
The shipped bundle still constructs this route through Yammer-named helper code and can add `X-Yammer-ThirdPartyCookieBlocked` when that condition is detected.
The helper request was captured directly from the default Edge profile during the authenticated admin landing flow.

operations.summary: Open a Viva Engage realtime relay session

workflows: Open `https://engage.cloud.microsoft/main/admin` or a validated admin sub-route and allow `RealtimeConnectionSettingsClients` to return the transient `cometdBaseUrl`.
Reuse that `cometdBaseUrl` host to post a Bayeux handshake to `/cometd/handshake`.

notes: The relay host is transient and was observed changing across page loads.
The handshake request carried redacted auth material in the body and did not include `Authorization` or cookie headers.
The default Edge profile captured the handshake immediately after `RealtimeConnectionSettingsClients` returned `cometdBaseUrl`.

operations.summary: Subscribe to Viva Engage realtime relay channels

workflows: Complete the Bayeux handshake against the relay host returned by `RealtimeConnectionSettingsClients`.
Post one or more `/meta/subscribe` envelopes to `/cometd/` for the desired user, feed, or broadcast channels.

notes: The observed subscription set was tenant- and user-scoped.
Captured subscribe posts continued to rely on the relay `clientId` instead of `Authorization` or cookie headers.
The default Edge profile captured successful relay subscriptions after the handshake completed.

operations.summary: Poll the Viva Engage realtime relay

workflows: Complete the Bayeux handshake and any required subscriptions against the relay host returned by `RealtimeConnectionSettingsClients`.
Post `/meta/connect` envelopes to `/cometd/connect` to maintain the realtime channel.

notes: The captured connect requests continued the relay session without `Authorization` or cookie headers.
The successful response returned relay advice such as reconnect timing on earlier connect cycles.
The default Edge profile captured repeated successful `/meta/connect` long-polls after the relay subscriptions were established.
